senses.definition: a main part of a play, opera, or ballet

examples.x: The first act of the play introduces the main characters and their conflicts.
Each act of the opera showcased the talents of the performers.
The final act of the ballet featured an impressive display of choreography.
The actors rehearsed diligently to perfect their performances for each act.
After the intermission, the audience eagerly anticipated the second act.

senses.definition: to change a book or play in a way that can be made into a movie, TV series, etc.

examples.x: The screenwriter adapted the bestselling novel into a screenplay.
The director worked closely with the playwright to adapt the stage production for television.
The producers hired a team of writers to adapt the classic play for a modern audience.
The screenwriter successfully adapted the nonfiction book into a gripping true-crime series.
The studio acquired the rights to adapt the graphic novel for TV.

senses.definition: a form of performing art that narrates a story using complex dance movements set to music but no words

examples.x: The ballet performance captivated the audience with its elegant choreography and beautiful music.
She has been studying ballet since she was a child, dreaming of becoming a professional dancer.
He attended the ballet with his family to appreciate the beauty and athleticism of the dancers.
She enrolled in ballet classes to improve her posture and flexibility.
Ballet performances often feature elaborate sets and costumes to enhance the storytelling through dance.

senses.definition: a thing that achieves great widespread popularity or financial success, particularly a movie, book, or other product

examples.x: The new superhero movie became a blockbuster, earning over $1 billion at the box office.
The author's latest novel was a literary blockbuster, topping bestseller lists worldwide.

senses.definition: the total income a movie makes by selling tickets

examples.x: The new Marvel movie topped the box office charts on its opening weekend.
The musical broke box office records, selling out every performance for months.
The comedy film exceeded expectations at the box office, earning over $100 million worldwide.
The theater director was thrilled with the play's box office performance, which surpassed projections.

senses.definition: to take part in a play, TV show, movie, etc.

examples.x: The famous actor will appear in the upcoming blockbuster film.
She will appear as a guest judge on the reality TV show next week.
The beloved character will appear in the final season of the television series.
He is set to appear in a supporting role in the Broadway play next month.
The talk show host will appear as a guest star on the sitcom's season finale.

senses.definition: to choose a performer to play a role in a movie, opera, play, etc.

examples.x: The director will cast the lead role in the upcoming musical next week.
They cast several actors from different backgrounds to bring diversity to the film.
The producer cast him as the villain due to his strong performance in auditions.
The theater company cast a famous actress for the main role in the play.

senses.definition: to make a movie, image, etc. appear on a flat surface from afar

examples.x: The technician will project the video clips for the audience to see.
He is projecting the latest movie at the film festival right now.
The teacher will project a slideshow during the class presentation.
They plan to project the images during the art exhibition to enhance the experience.

senses.definition: to show a video or film in a movie theater or on TV

examples.x: The cinema will screen the highly anticipated movie this weekend.
The television network will screen the documentary on environmental conservation next week.
The film festival will screen a selection of independent movies from various genres.
The local community center plans to screen classic films for residents every month.
The streaming service will screen the latest episodes of the popular TV series.

senses.definition: the rebroadcast of a program on television or other media

examples.x: The cable channel airs reruns of old movies every weekend.
The rerun of the live concert drew a large audience eager to relive the experience.
The network scheduled a rerun of last season's finale due to popular demand.
She caught a rerun of her favorite cooking show while waiting at the airport.

senses.definition: a showing of a movie to an audience, typically at a scheduled time

examples.x: I missed the first screening, so I’ll catch the next one at 8 PM.
We went to the early screening of the new superhero movie.
There's a special screening of a classic film tonight at the local cinema.
The film festival offers multiple screenings of each movie throughout the week.
They hosted a private screening for the cast and crew before the official release.
After the screening, there was a Q&A session with the director.

senses.definition: to film or take a photograph of something
to present a play or other event to an audience

examples.x: The theater company stages Shakespearean plays throughout the year.
They stage a musical production every spring.
The theater group plans to stage a new production of Romeo and Juliet this summer.
The director is known for staging elaborate and visually stunning plays.
The opera will be staged at the historic downtown theater.

senses.definition: someone who loves movies very much

examples.x: As a true cinephile, she attends film festivals every year to discover new talent.
His friends often rely on him for recommendations since he is a cinephile.
The cinephile spent hours exploring classic films from different eras.
The bookstore has a section dedicated to cinephiles, featuring books about film theory and history.

senses.definition: (of images or drawings in a movie) made to appear as if they are in motion

examples.x: The animated movie was a hit with children and adults alike.
The animated short film won several awards at the festival.
She made an animated short film for her art project.

senses.definition: a short part of a movie or broadcast that is viewed separately

examples.x: The director shared a clip from the film during the promotional event.
She watched a funny clip of the comedian's latest stand-up routine.
The news program included a clip of the interview with the celebrity.
He edited a clip of his favorite scenes to share on social media.

senses.definition: a few words or actions that hint another performer to say a line or do something

examples.x: The actor waited for his cue before entering the stage.
The director gave a subtle nod as a cue for the lights to dim.
She missed her cue and entered the scene at the wrong moment.
His cue came just as the audience held their breath in anticipation.
During rehearsals, the actors practiced responding to each other's cues.

senses.definition: a person who substitutes for an actor in a film, typically during scenes that involves nude or dangerous scenes
a dangerous and difficult action that shows great skill and is done to entertain people, typically as part of a movie

examples.x: The actor performed his own stunt during the action scene, impressing the crew.
The movie featured a thrilling stunt where the car jumped over a ramp.
She trained for months to master the complex stunt for her upcoming film.
The director was pleased with how the stunt added excitement to the storyline.
Safety measures are crucial in the planning and execution of any stunt.

senses.definition: a technician who is in charge of the lights in a TV or motion picture set or theater stage

examples.x: The lighting engineer worked closely with the director to create the perfect atmosphere for the play.
During the concert, the lighting engineer coordinated the lights to match the music's rhythm.
The film's success was partly due to the creative work of the lighting engineer.
Collaborating with other technicians, the lighting engineer ensured that everything was ready before the show started.

senses.definition: someone whose job is to take care of the business affairs of an actor, musician, sports player, etc.

examples.x: The manager arranged a meeting between the actor and the director for the upcoming film.
She hired a manager to help promote her music career and book performances.
The sports player's manager negotiated a lucrative contract with the team.
His manager advised him on the best courses to take for his acting career.
During the press tour, the manager ensured that the musician stayed on schedule.

senses.definition: a person whose job is to write scripts for movies, TV series, etc.

examples.x: The screenwriter spent months developing the script for the upcoming movie.
She is a talented screenwriter known for her work on several successful TV series.
The screenwriter's adaptation of the novel impressed critics and audiences alike.
He started his career as a screenwriter, writing scripts for independent films.
The screenwriter attended a workshop to learn more about writing dialogue for screenplays.

senses.definition: the first public presentation of a play, musical, movie, or any other form of entertainment

examples.x: The opening of the Broadway musical was eagerly anticipated by theater enthusiasts.
They attended the opening of the art exhibition to see the new works by local artists.
The film received positive reviews after its opening at the film festival.
Actors and crew members celebrated the successful opening of the play with a reception.
The orchestra received a standing ovation at the opening of their concert series.

senses.definition: the showing of a movie, play, exhibition, etc. to a selected audience before its public release

examples.x: The film studio held a preview for critics before the movie's official release.
We attended the preview of the new art exhibition at the gallery.
The play had a preview performance to gather audience feedback.
A special preview of the upcoming season aired on television last night.
The preview of the new video game generated excitement among fans.

senses.definition: a motion picture, TV program, etc. that is created for the public view

examples.x: She was thrilled to land a role in a major production after months of auditions.
Filming for the new production began in the spring and wrapped up in the fall.
The award-winning production captivated audiences with its stunning visuals.
The latest production from the studio received rave reviews from critics.

senses.definition: a session of practice in which performers prepare themselves for a public performance of a concert, play, etc.

examples.x: The actors gathered for their final rehearsal before the opening night of the play.
During rehearsal, the cast worked on blocking scenes to enhance stage movement.
The singer's vocal coach joined her for a private rehearsal to refine her technique.
The theater director scheduled additional rehearsals to address timing issues in the comedy.
The band members practiced tirelessly during rehearsal to synchronize their musical cues.

senses.definition: to practice a play, piece of music, etc. before the public performance

examples.x: The actors gathered in the theater to rehearse their lines and perfect their stage movements before the opening night.
The musicians decided to rehearse their new song several times to ensure a flawless performance.
Before the big dance recital, the ballet troupe spent hours rehearsing their routines.
The theater cast diligently rehearsed their scenes to deliver a polished and engaging performance.
The choir members dedicated extra time to rehearse their harmonies for the upcoming concert.

senses.definition: the industry or profession of providing public entertainment, such as motion picture, theater, etc.

examples.x: The awards ceremony celebrated the achievements of those in show business.
Many aspiring actors dream of making it big in show business.
She attended a workshop to learn more about the ins and outs of show business.
Networking is crucial for success in show business, helping artists connect with industry professionals.
The rise of streaming services has changed the landscape of show business significantly.

senses.definition: a written description of the characters, events, or settings in a movie or play

examples.x: The screenwriter presented several scenarios for the plot of the upcoming movie.
Military strategists developed multiple scenarios to anticipate different outcomes of the battle.
She imagined various scenarios for her future career path before making a decision.
The novel explores a dystopian scenario where society has collapsed due to environmental catastrophe.

senses.definition: transcribed or translated words of the narrative or dialogues of a movie or TV show, appearing at the bottom of the screen to help deaf people or those who do not understand the language

examples.x: The foreign film had subtitles in English to help viewers understand the dialogue.
She turned on the subtitles while watching the documentary to catch every detail.
The DVD includes subtitles in multiple languages for international audiences.
Viewers with hearing impairments rely on subtitles to enjoy movies and TV shows.
The streaming platform allows users to customize subtitle settings for font size and color.

senses.definition: an artificial sound created and used in a motion picture, play, video game, etc. to make it more realistic

examples.x: The sound effect of thunder added tension to the dramatic scene in the movie.
During the play, the sound effect of birds chirping helped create a serene atmosphere.
The sound effect of a door creaking made the horror film even more frightening.
Editors carefully selected sound effects to match the action on screen.
Video game designers use sound effects to immerse players in the gaming experience.

senses.definition: a machine that is used in a theater or movie to create artificial wind

examples.x: The wind machine created a dramatic effect during the outdoor scene of the movie.
Photographers used a wind machine to add movement to the model's hair for the shoot.
The theater production employed a wind machine to enhance the storm scene on stage.
Directors often rely on wind machines to make scenes feel more realistic and dynamic.
The sound of the wind machine added to the atmosphere of the haunting film.
